name: ghz (gRPC Benchmarking Tool)

auth.notes: TLS, mTLS, custom metadata headers for auth

performance.notes: Go-based; concurrent gRPC client with configurable concurrency

agent_readiness.notes: gRPC load testing and benchmarking. ghz --insecure --proto helloworld.proto --call helloworld.Greeter.SayHello -d '{"name":"World"}' -n 10000 -c 100 localhost:50051. -n requests, -c concurrency, -q QPS. Output formats: summary, CSV, JSON, HTML, influx, pretty. Streaming: client/server/bidirectional. Config file support. Metadata: -m '{"token":"xxx"}'. The gRPC equivalent of k6/vegeta for load testing.

gotchas: Complex data patterns need config file rather than CLI flags
No scripting for multi-step gRPC workflows
best_when: gRPC service load testing and benchmarking
avoid_when: When grpcurl suffices for simple manual testing
summary: ghz benchmarks gRPC services with configurable concurrency and QPS. ghz --insecure --proto service.proto --call pkg.Service.Method -d '{...}' -n 10000 -c 100 host:port. JSON/HTML/CSV/InfluxDB output. Streaming RPC support. Config file for complex setups. The vegeta/k6 equivalent for gRPC load testing. Requires proto files or server reflection.
